title: The "AI suggests, you decide" model - is this the future of AI-assisted trading?
author: Upbeat_Cabinet_9628
selftext:
Been thinking about this a lot. Most AI trading tools promise to do everything for you - "let AI beat the market while you sleep." But that creates a fundamental problem: who's responsible when things go wrong?

The more I look at this space, the more I believe the human-in-the-loop approach makes more sense. AI can:

\- Process massive datasets

\- Identify patterns

\- Generate signals

 

But humans should:

\- Interpret context

\- Make judgment calls

\- Maintain accountability

 

There's been some discussion about a platform called MOTA that's built around this principle. Multiple AI agents working together, but humans making final decisions. The concept of "Manager of Trading Agents" - orchestrating different specialized agents rather than relying on one black-box model.

Still early days, but this direction feels more aligned with how professional trading actually works. AI as augmentation, not replacement.

Curious if others here have thoughts on the human-in-the-loop approach vs fully automated systems.
